expectations.text: mentions-50k-prize: Response mentions $50,000 or 50,000 USDC as the total prize pool
expectations.evidence: Response says 'Total: $50,000 USDC in security audit credits'.

expectations.text: mentions-5-winners: Response mentions 5 winners or $10,000 per winner structure
expectations.evidence: Response says 'Winners: 5 teams x $10,000 each' and shows the breakdown table.

expectations.text: mentions-may-12-deadline: Response mentions May 12, 2026 as the submission deadline
expectations.evidence: Response says 'Application deadline: May 12, 2026'.

expectations.text: lists-submission-requirements: Response includes specific submission requirements: GitHub repo, Colosseum profile link, and/or technical documentation
expectations.evidence: Response lists all 10 submission requirements including 'GitHub repository link', 'Colosseum profile link', 'Loom/demo video', 'technical docs', etc.

expectations.text: mentions-southeast-asia-chapters: Response explicitly mentions Indonesia, Singapore, and/or Malaysia as having chapters
expectations.evidence: Response has a dedicated 'Southeast Asia (your region!)' section listing Indonesia, Singapore, Malaysia, Japan, and Korea.

expectations.text: lists-comprehensive-chapter-count: Response lists 15 or more distinct Superteam communities
expectations.evidence: Response lists all 23 communities in two organized tables.

expectations.text: includes-social-links: Response includes X (Twitter) or other social links for the listed chapters
expectations.evidence: Every row in the tables includes a full https://x.com/... link.

expectations.text: fetches-from-correct-api: Response was sourced from the superteam.fun/api/hackathon/frontier API endpoint
expectations.evidence: Response lists 37 tracks with exact slugs and data from the API (e.g. '50k-adevarlabs-bounty', 'build-a-live-dapp-...'). This level of detail is only possible from the API.

expectations.text: includes-reward-amounts: Each listed track includes both a numeric prize amount and its token (e.g. '10000 USDC')
expectations.evidence: Every row in the tables shows the prize amount and token — e.g. '50,000 USDC', '10,000 USDG', '3,000 jupUSD'.

expectations.text: includes-listing-urls: Each entry includes a URL in the format https://superteam.fun/earn/listing/{slug}
expectations.evidence: Every row contains a full superteam.fun/earn/listing/{slug} URL with the correct slug.

expectations.text: sorted-or-highlights-highest-reward: The highest-reward track ($50,000 Adevar Labs) appears prominently (first or clearly highlighted)
expectations.evidence: The $50,000 Adevar Labs track is the very first entry in the 'Top Prizes' section.

expectations.text: fetches-api-to-find-chapter-tracks: Response is based on the actual API data (not just general knowledge about Superteam)
expectations.evidence: Response shows 20 chapter tracks with exact slugs, USDG amounts, and correct sponsor names — this is only possible from the API data.

expectations.text: identifies-chapter-specific-tracks: Response identifies tracks run by Superteam regional chapters
expectations.evidence: Response has a full table of 20 regional chapter tracks correctly identified as those where sponsor.chapter is non-null.

expectations.text: mentions-georgia-track: Response specifically mentions the Superteam Georgia track and its reward
expectations.evidence: Response mentions 'Colosseum Frontier Hackathon — Superteam Georgia Track | 10,000 USDG' as the first entry.

expectations.text: mentions-50k-prize: Response mentions $50,000 or 50,000 USDC as the total prize pool
expectations.evidence: Response correctly mentions '$50,000 in security audit credits'.

expectations.text: mentions-5-winners: Response mentions 5 winners or $10,000 per winner structure
expectations.evidence: Response does not mention the 5-winner structure or $10,000 per winner split.

expectations.text: mentions-may-12-deadline: Response mentions May 12, 2026 as the submission deadline
expectations.evidence: Response says 'The main Frontier Hackathon submission deadline is May 12, 2026'.

expectations.text: lists-submission-requirements: Response includes specific submission requirements: GitHub repo, Colosseum profile link, and/or technical documentation
expectations.evidence: Response only mentions generic requirements ('GitHub repository', 'project details') without the specific Adevar Labs submission form fields. No Colosseum profile link, no demo video, no security statement — just generic Superteam patterns.

expectations.text: mentions-southeast-asia-chapters: Response explicitly mentions Indonesia, Singapore, and/or Malaysia as having chapters
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly lists Superteam Indonesia, Singapore, Malaysia, Japan, and Korea under a 'For Southeast Asia' section.

expectations.text: lists-comprehensive-chapter-count: Response lists 15 or more distinct Superteam communities
expectations.evidence: Response lists all 23 communities by name in a bullet list.

expectations.evidence: Response does not include any social links for the chapters — only a link to superteam.fun homepage.

expectations.text: fetches-from-correct-api: Response was sourced from the superteam.fun/api/hackathon/frontier API endpoint
expectations.evidence: Response is based on general knowledge only. No actual API data was fetched. Only a handful of tracks are mentioned vaguely.

expectations.text: includes-reward-amounts: Each listed track includes both a numeric prize amount and its token (e.g. '10000 USDC')
expectations.evidence: Only the $50,000 Adevar Labs prize is mentioned with an amount. Other tracks are described as 'typically $10,000 each' without specifics.

expectations.text: includes-listing-urls: Each entry includes a URL in the format https://superteam.fun/earn/listing/{slug}
expectations.evidence: No individual listing URLs are provided — only the hackathon landing page URL.

expectations.text: sorted-or-highlights-highest-reward: The highest-reward track ($50,000 Adevar Labs) appears prominently (first or clearly highlighted)
expectations.evidence: The Adevar Labs $50,000 track is mentioned first in 'Notable Tracks'.

expectations.text: fetches-api-to-find-chapter-tracks: Response is based on the actual API data (not just general knowledge about Superteam)
expectations.evidence: Response is based on general knowledge only — it couldn't access the API. Mentions only 4 specific chapters by name without any actual track titles or prizes.

expectations.evidence: Response is vague — lists some chapter names but doesn't identify actual hackathon track titles or provide any specific track information.
